behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is real git rename detection as observed by the CLI on an actual repository. Mocking git output or filesystem state can miss real rename heuristics and status-format edge cases, so a real repo + CLI execution is the minimum to catch true failures. Higher levels are disqualified because no AI/ML call or multi-agent pipeline exists in the behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert the report increments only the rename category (not added/deleted for the same path).

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git diff behavior for binary files and real CLI execution handling that output. In-process stubs for diff content can hide failures tied to actual git binary markers/exit behavior, so System is the minimum. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no model output quality or multi-step agentic decision chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI process, assert exit code is success, no crash, and binary-related change count is present in the structured stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs at the boundary between CLI and real git repository state (unborn HEAD/no commits), which is an OS/subprocess/repo-state condition. Mocking the git layer may not reproduce exact error modes from real git, so System is the minimum. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because there is no AI call.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, execute the CLI, and assert successful exit with an empty structured report instead of an error message/traceback.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history semantics across multiple change types. The real failure is mismatch between actual repository history and CLI output; mocks can encode the same mistaken assumptions as implementation and miss regressions. System is required; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events over several commits, run the CLI, and assert reported category counts exactly match counts independently computed from git history ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: The behavior targets parity across real index vs working-tree states, which is a git state-machine boundary observable through actual repo/index operations and CLI invocation. Fakes often flatten these distinctions and can hide real failures, so System is the minimum. Higher levels are disqualified: no AI model call and no agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI in each state, normalize nondeterministic fields if any, and assert identical structured stdout output.
